north_star_verdict.headline: Early-stage deal has gone cold: no buyer touch since Anggita Kristina's 5 May 2026 email (62 days), the mid-April ANA demo and buyer use cases were never delivered, and the mandatory Indonesia data-center localization check flagged 27 April is still unresolved. Ownership just moved to Carl Kimball on 1 July, so the relationship must be rebuilt from a standing start against a December close that no longer has a credible path. Coupa remains the incumbent and no economic buyer or champion is confirmed.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 12 May 2026 (55 days old), Qualified since creation with zero stage progression. Close 1 Dec 2026 is 148 days out. LastActivityDate 11 May and last genuine buyer touch 5 May 2026 are both 62+ days ago, past the 60-day cold threshold. Milestones long overdue: demo due mid-April (11+ weeks late), buyer use cases due mid-April (never delivered), Indonesia DC compliance flagged 27 April (unresolved 10+ weeks). Owner reassigned to Carl Kimball on 1 Jul 2026. On this pace, reaching Shortlisted or Vendor Selected before a December close is not credible without an immediate re-engagement.

metrics.narrative: No quantified business case exists. The Next_Step trail references 'Early initiative around spend analysis, AppXtend' with 'Use cases to be shared by mid of April' (24 March 2026), but the buyer never delivered those use cases, so scope, savings target, cycle-time impact and ROI remain undefined. The 27 April 2026 rep note frames a 'Larger Ai initiative in Sinar Mas conglomerate' as the umbrella but attaches no numbers. Until Anggita Kristina or a procurement lead shares the promised use cases, we cannot size the value, so obtaining them and quantifying the AI/spend-analysis impact is the prerequisite move before any demo or commercial step.

Next_Step__c 27 April 2026: 'AI appetite. Larger Ai initiative in Sinar Mas conglomerate'
Next_Step__c 13 March 2026: 'Interest in our ANA capabilities'

economic_buyer.narrative: No economic buyer is confirmed in Salesforce: there are zero OpportunityContactRole records and no budget-owner field is populated. The strongest org-chart clue is the 24 March 2026 Next_Step, 'Meeting with Head of Strategic Procurement Sinar Mas', which points to that Head of Strategic Procurement as the likely budget authority, but no such person is named on a Salesforce contact or activity this sweep, so we cannot attribute a name or title. Anggita Kristina (Corporate Procurement, our single thread) is an influencer, not the budget owner. Identifying and getting to the Head of Strategic Procurement is a top move.

decision_criteria.narrative: Two criteria are visible from the Next_Step trail: AI/ANA capability ('Interest in our ANA capabilities', 13 March 2026) and spend analysis plus AppXtend extensibility ('Early initiative around spend analysis, AppXtend', 24 March 2026). A hard gating criterion is Indonesia data-center localization, flagged 27 April 2026 as 'mandatory for Indo', which any vendor must satisfy to be viable locally. These are buyer-referenced but not yet validated in a demo or requirements document, so criteria are directional rather than confirmed; the promised use cases would firm them up.

decision_process.narrative: The decision process is largely unknown. The only sequence signal is the 4 May 2026 introductory call (SF event 'Sinarmas Mining <> Zycus : Introductory Call') followed by an email exchange around demo scheduling, then 62 days of silence, so we have not yet mapped who evaluates, who approves, or the approval chain. The 20 March 2026 note that this 'Can be part of a broader S2P opportunity at Sinar Mas conglomerate (SG/Indonesia)' suggests a multi-entity, conglomerate-level process that could involve stakeholders beyond the Mining subsidiary. Reconstructing the actual evaluation and approval path is required to forecast anything.

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are entirely undiscussed: no MSA, SOW, legal, procurement channel or InfoSec conversation appears in any field, task or next step, which is expected at Qualified stage. The one paper-relevant forcing factor is the Indonesia data-center localization requirement (27 April 2026, 'mandatory for Indo'), which is a compliance gate that must be cleared before contracting and likely involves local partner TechConnect (fernando.natanael@techconnect.co.id). Visibility will only come once a demo and business case advance the deal toward evaluation.

identify_pain.narrative: Qualitative pain is confirmed but not quantified. The buyer is 'currently using Coupa' and expressed 'Interest in our ANA capabilities' (13 March 2026), implying a gap in Coupa's AI-driven sourcing/negotiation that Zycus ANA addresses. The 24 March 2026 'spend analysis, AppXtend' reference points to pain around spend visibility and application extensibility, and the 27 April 2026 'Larger Ai initiative' frames a strategic imperative to modernize procurement with AI. No operational or financial impact (cost, cycle time, headcount, error rate) has been sized. The pain owner is the procurement function, but no individual owner is confirmed and the buyer has been silent 62 days.
sources: Next_Step__c 13 March 2026: 'currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities'
Next_Step__c 24 March 2026: 'spend analysis, AppXtend'
Next_Step__c 27 April 2026: 'AI appetite. Larger Ai initiative in Sinar Mas conglomerate'

champion.narrative: No champion is identified. Anggita Kristina (Corporate Procurement) is the sole active thread and sent the last buyer email on 5 May 2026, but she has shown no advocacy behavior, no introductions to power, no internal documents shared, and no agenda-driving, and she has been silent for 62 days. There is no evidence she can reach the Head of Strategic Procurement or the conglomerate AI-initiative sponsor. The owner's own 10% confidence (27 April 2026) is consistent with having no champion. With the deal now reassigned to Carl Kimball on 1 July, the priority is to re-engage Anggita, test her willingness to advocate, and if she stays passive, thread to a senior procurement sponsor.
sources: Last buyer email 5 May 2026 from Anggita Kristina (Clari/EmailMessage)
Zero OpportunityContactRole for Champion
Next_Step__c 27 April 2026: '10% confidence'

competition.narrative: Coupa is the incumbent, confirmed in the 13 March 2026 Next_Step: 'currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities.' That interest is our wedge: the buyer is looking past Coupa for AI-native negotiation and spend intelligence that Coupa's platform does not deliver, so Zycus ANA and Merlin are the differentiator. No other competitor is named in any source. Displacing an incumbent requires a quantified value case, a champion and a delivered demo, none of which exist yet, and 62 days of silence means we have made no progress against Coupa since the 4 May intro call. Threat level is medium: Coupa holds switching-cost and adoption advantages, but the buyer's stated ANA interest signals genuine openness to a better AI alternative.
sources: Next_Step__c 13 March 2026: 'currently using Coupa. Interest in our ANA capabilities'
Products__c: Agentic AI;ANA (our differentiation)

deal_movement.summary: The deal has stalled since the 4 May 2026 introductory call: the buyer went silent after the 5 May demo-scheduling email, the mid-April demo and use cases were never delivered, and the only material movement since is an internal ownership change to Carl Kimball on 1 July 2026. Stage has not moved off Qualified since creation.

competitive_position.summary: Coupa is the sole named competitor and the incumbent, confirmed 13 March 2026 ('currently using Coupa'). It is the single strongest current threat by default, holding switching-cost and adoption advantages, but the buyer's stated interest in Zycus ANA is a real AI wedge Coupa does not fill. Threat level is medium and static: with 62 days of buyer silence and no demo delivered, nothing has moved the competitive picture since the 4 May intro call, so we have neither gained nor lost ground against Coupa.

Reads as AI Curious, not yet AI Hungry.

deal_scores_evidence.summary: A cold, single-threaded early-stage deal against incumbent Coupa: the buyer went silent after the 4 May intro call, never delivered the promised use cases or took the demo, and ownership just moved to Carl Kimball, so the only thing between us and a stall is a fast re-engagement that maps a real economic buyer.

win_position.text: We stand low: no economic buyer or champion is confirmed, the buyer has been silent 62 days since Anggita Kristina's 5 May email, and Coupa is the entrenched incumbent (Next_Step, 13 Mar).
Our one real opening is buyer-stated interest in Zycus ANA capabilities against a conglomerate-wide AI initiative (rep note, 27 Apr), a gap Coupa does not fill.
The AI thesis is unproven because the mid-April demo was never delivered and the buyer's promised spend-analysis use cases never arrived, so there is no quantified value case.
Mandatory Indonesia data-center localization (flagged 27 Apr) is an unresolved gate that could disqualify us before commercials.

deal_momentum.text: Zero stage progression since creation on 12 May and no buyer-facing deliverable in 60+ days; the deal is effectively frozen.
The only recent movement is internal: ownership reassigned to Carl Kimball on 1 July, which resets the relationship rather than advancing it.

customer_commitment.text: Single-threaded through influencer Anggita Kristina with no signed next step, no named economic buyer, and the buyer defaulting on both the use cases and demo scheduling.

deal_risk.text: 62-day silence plus a December close date makes the timeline the primary risk; the date is no longer credible without immediate re-engagement.
Displacing incumbent Coupa with no champion, no value case, and an unresolved localization requirement is a steep, high-risk path from Qualified.

critical_signals.text: The 4 May introductory call produced no follow-on: the buyer stopped replying after the 5 May demo-scheduling email and the demo was never delivered.
Coupa is the entrenched incumbent, and the buyer's interest in Zycus ANA is our only wedge, which has gone quiet since March.
The account was reassigned to new owner Carl Kimball on 1 July, so the buyer relationship must be rebuilt before any progress is possible.
No pricing or ROI has been discussed and the buyer never shared the promised spend-analysis use cases, so the business case is undefined.
